name: Blood
tagline: Manipulate blood to harm, heal, or empower creatures.

baseAbility.name: Blood Control
baseAbility.description: Standard action, close range, ranged touch attack against a living creature (or one that's bled/been blood-drained recently). While under your blood control, the target can be made to bleed (damage each round equal to your CL, stoppable by Heal check, healing, or your Coagulate option) or to stop bleeding/heal a little. Lasts as long as you concentrate.

talents.name: Hemokinesis
talents.description: Blood control can be delivered as a ranged attack instead of only melee touch.

talents.name: Mark of Blood
talents.description: A struck target is marked by your blood, easier to affect with further Blood sphere effects (treated as below half HP for save-penalty purposes, ignores their spell resistance) until they wipe it off.

talents.name: Extract Blood Construct
talents.description: Solidify blood into a temporary blood construct creature that fights for you.

talents.name: Inject
talents.description: Force a poison, drug, or drawn substance into a blood-controlled target's bloodstream instead of dealing bleed damage.

talents.name: Mass Control
talents.description: Apply blood control to 1 extra target per 2 CL (min 1) at once.

talents.name: Blood Arc
talents.description: Arc blood from a bleeding target to blind a nearby creature on a failed Reflex save.

talents.name: Hemorrhage
talents.description: Your bleed damage die size increases, dealing more damage per round to blood-controlled targets.

talents.name: Coagulate Mastery
talents.description: Your Coagulate option (stopping bleeding, healing a little) becomes more potent and can remove ability damage tied to blood loss.

talents.name: Control Oxygenation
talents.description: Suffocate a blood-controlled target, or push them into overdrive for bonus actions at the cost of Constitution burn.

talents.name: Manipulate Alchemy
talents.description: Alter the potency, delivery, or duration of poisons and alchemical items already in a blood-controlled target's system.
